edges.description: Bright red facial erythema confined to the cheeks, giving the appearance of having been slapped.

nodes.description: Classic childhood exanthem characterized by facial erythema (slapped cheek) followed by lacy reticular rash on trunk and extremities.

evidence.snippet: Erythema infectiosum, the most common clinical manifestation of parvovirus B19 infection, is characterized by a 'slapped cheek' appearance on the face and lacy exanthem on the trunk and extremities.

evidence.reference_title: The cutaneous manifestations of human parvovirus B19 infection.
evidence.snippet: The prototypical cutaneous manifestations of human parvovirus B19 (B19) infection include a petechial eruption in a glove and stocking distribution, reticular truncal erythema, and the 'slapped cheek' sign.

evidence.reference_title: Erythema infectiosum and other parvovirus B19 infections.
evidence.snippet: The human parvovirus B19 has been identified as the causative agent in several diseases, including the benign childhood condition erythema infectiosum (fifth disease)...

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric parvovirus B19: spectrum of clinical manifestations.
evidence.snippet: The most common manifestation of PVB19 infection in children is erythema infectiosum (EI). Also known as fifth disease or slapped cheek syndrome, EI presents as an erythematous exanthem limited to the malar eminences that follows a mild prodromal illness.

nodes.description: Bright red malar erythema giving the face a slapped appearance, pathognomonic for erythema infectiosum.
